1	"""Gate-A no-spend dry-run harness (I-meta-002 sub-PR-6).
2	
3	This is the operator's NO-SPEND green gate: it proves the 4-role substrate is internally
4	consistent and exercisable OFFLINE, with NO real network and NO money, BEFORE the operator
5	authorizes any paid (Gate-B) run. It emits a machine-readable PASS/FAIL report plus a
6	human-readable summary for the dual §-1.1 line-by-line review.
7	
8	The four PASS checks (ALL must be green; ALL no-spend):
9	
10	  (a) pytest tests/roles tests/architecture tests/dr_benchmark — run SERIALIZED (one process
11	      at a time per CLAUDE.md §8.4 resource discipline), each rc captured.
12	  (b) ``verify_lock --consistency`` exit-code 0 — the architecture lock is internally
13	      consistent (families registered, all-distinct policy holds, code defaults match, the
14	      canonical pin includes the lock) REGARDLESS of the pending ``status`` field.
15	  (c) FROZEN-LOCK COVERAGE (Codex P2 option (b)): build the FOUR role pins from the
16	      lock-sourced ``pathB_runner._role_pins()`` and run ``validate_role_families`` on the
17	      effective map — assert generator/mirror/sentinel/judge are present AND their families are
18	      all distinct. This DELIBERATELY does NOT call ``_assert_architecture_coverage`` (which
19	      RAISES while the lock is pending): there is no bypass mode a paid smoke could reuse.
20	  (d) Per-role contract fixtures via the MOCK transport: Sentinel ``yes`` => UNGROUNDED
21	      (lethal-polarity), Judge 5-enum (off-enum raises), Mirror two-pass binding holds.
22	
23	The 3 cheap real probes (Serper / Semantic Scholar / DeepSeek) are GATED behind a default-OFF
24	``--with-live-probes`` flag and are NEVER part of the PASS criteria — the dry run is pure
25	zero-spend unless the operator explicitly opts in. Even with the flag set, the probes are
26	ADVISORY telemetry only.
27	
28	Import-safety: importing this module performs NO I/O and starts NO subprocess. argparse,
29	subprocess, and pytest invocation all live under ``if __name__ == "__main__"`` / the
30	``main()`` entry only; the check functions are pure enough to be unit-tested offline (the
31	coverage + contract checks take no network).
32	"""
33	
34	from __future__ import annotations
35	
36	import json
37	import subprocess
38	import sys
39	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
40	from pathlib import Path
41	
42	# Repo root: <root>/scripts/dr_benchmark/gate_a_dry_run.py -> parents[2].
43	_REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2]
44	
45	# The four locked roles the coverage check must observe (canonical pipeline order).
46	_EXPECTED_ROLES = ("generator", "mirror", "sentinel", "judge")
47	
48	# The pytest suites the dry run runs serialized (no-spend, offline).
49	_PYTEST_SUITES = ("tests/roles", "tests/architecture", "tests/dr_benchmark")
50	
51	# Default report path under the repo's outputs tree.
52	_DEFAULT_REPORT_PATH = _REPO_ROOT / "outputs" / "gate_a" / "dry_run_report.json"
53	
54	# Mock-transport canned outputs for the per-role contract fixtures (check (d)).
55	_SENTINEL_YES = "<score>yes</score>"  # yes = risk present = UNGROUNDED (lethal-polarity guard)
56	_JUDGE_OFF_ENUM = "definitely-true"   # off-enum token -> JudgeEnumError (no silent default)
57	
58	
59	@dataclass
60	class CheckResult:
61	    """One Gate-A check outcome."""
62	
63	    name: str
64	    passed: bool
65	    detail: str
66	
67	
68	@dataclass
69	class GateAReport:
70	    """The full Gate-A dry-run report."""
71	
72	    overall_pass: bool
73	    checks: list[CheckResult] = field(default_factory=list)
74	    live_probes_ran: bool = False
75	    live_probe_results: list[CheckResult] = field(default_factory=list)
76	
77	    def to_json_dict(self) -> dict:
78	        return {
79	            "overall_pass": self.overall_pass,
80	            "checks": [
81	                {"name": c.name, "passed": c.passed, "detail": c.detail}
82	                for c in self.checks
83	            ],
84	            "live_probes_ran": self.live_probes_ran,
85	            "live_probe_results": [
86	                {"name": c.name, "passed": c.passed, "detail": c.detail}
87	                for c in self.live_probe_results
88	            ],
89	            "no_spend": True,
90	            "note": (
91	                "Gate-A is no-spend/offline. PASS does NOT authorize a paid run; lock "
92	                "promotion to status: locked is the operator's separate spend gate (Gate-B)."
93	            ),
94	        }
95	
96	
97	# --- check (a): pytest suites, serialized -------------------------------------------------
98	def check_pytest_suites(suites: tuple[str, ...] = _PYTEST_SUITES) -> CheckResult:
99	    """Run each pytest suite SERIALIZED (one subprocess at a time), capture rc.
100	
101	    PASS iff every suite exits 0. Serialized per CLAUDE.md §8.4 (no parallel pytest runs).
102	    """
103	    failures: list[str] = []
104	    for suite in suites:
105	        suite_path = _REPO_ROOT / suite
106	        if not suite_path.exists():
107	            failures.append(f"{suite}: MISSING")
108	            continue
109	        proc = subprocess.run(
110	            [sys.executable, "-m", "pytest", suite, "-q"],
111	            cwd=_REPO_ROOT,
112	            capture_output=True,
113	            text=True,
114	        )
115	        if proc.returncode != 0:
116	            tail = (proc.stdout or "").strip().splitlines()[-3:]
117	            failures.append(f"{suite}: rc={proc.returncode} {' | '.join(tail)}")
118	    if failures:
119	        return CheckResult("pytest_suites", False, "; ".join(failures))
120	    return CheckResult(
121	        "pytest_suites", True, f"all {len(suites)} suites passed (serialized)"
122	    )
123	
124	
125	# --- check (b): verify_lock --consistency rc==0 -------------------------------------------
126	def check_lock_consistency() -> CheckResult:
127	    """Run ``verify_lock --consistency`` and require exit-code 0 (status-independent)."""
128	    proc = subprocess.run(
129	        [sys.executable, "-m", "scripts.architecture.verify_lock", "--consistency"],
130	        cwd=_REPO_ROOT,
131	        capture_output=True,
132	        text=True,
133	    )
134	    detail = (proc.stdout or proc.stderr or "").strip().splitlines()
135	    detail_text = detail[-1] if detail else f"rc={proc.returncode}"
136	    return CheckResult("lock_consistency", proc.returncode == 0, detail_text)
137	
138	
139	# --- check (c): FROZEN-LOCK COVERAGE via P2 option (b) ------------------------------------
140	def check_frozen_lock_coverage() -> CheckResult:
141	    """Build the 4 role pins from the lock + run validate_role_families on the effective map.
142	
143	    Codex P2 option (b): call ``pathB_runner._role_pins()`` (lock-sourced) and
144	    ``openrouter_client.validate_role_families`` DIRECTLY. Assert the four roles
145	    generator/mirror/sentinel/judge are present AND their families are all distinct. This does
146	    NOT invoke ``_assert_architecture_coverage`` (which raises on a pending lock) and creates no
147	    bypass a paid smoke could reuse. No network: imports + pure pin-build + family registry.
148	    """
149	    # Imported lazily so the module stays import-safe at boot (no side effects on import).
150	    from src.polaris_graph.benchmark.pathB_runner import _role_pins
151	    from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import validate_role_families
152	
153	    pins = _role_pins()
154	    pinned_roles = {pin.role for pin in pins}
155	    # Assert EXACTLY the 4 locked roles — fail on a missing role OR an extra/stray pin (Codex
156	    # sub-PR-6 diff P2): the architecture-coverage invariant is set-equality, not just presence.
157	    expected = set(_EXPECTED_ROLES)
158	    if pinned_roles != expected:
159	        missing = sorted(expected - pinned_roles)
160	        extra = sorted(pinned_roles - expected)
161	        return CheckResult(
162	            "frozen_lock_coverage",
163	            False,
164	            f"role pins must be EXACTLY {sorted(expected)}; missing={missing} extra={extra} "
165	            f"observed={sorted(pinned_roles)}",
166	        )
167	
168	    effective_map = {pin.role: pin.model_slug for pin in pins}
169	    # Raises RuntimeError on an unknown family OR an all-distinct collision (fail loud).
170	    role_families = validate_role_families(effective_map)
171	    distinct_families = set(role_families.values())
172	    if len(distinct_families) != len(role_families):
173	        return CheckResult(
174	            "frozen_lock_coverage",
175	            False,
176	            f"family segregation violated: {role_families}",
177	        )
178	    return CheckResult(
179	        "frozen_lock_coverage",
180	        True,
181	        f"4 roles pinned + all-distinct families {role_families}",
182	    )
183	
184	
185	# --- check (d): per-role contract fixtures via the MOCK transport -------------------------
186	class _GateAMockTransport:
187	    """Mock RoleTransport for the offline per-role contract fixtures (no network, no spend).
188	
189	    Returns canned outputs keyed on role: Mirror two-pass grounded; Sentinel ``yes`` =>
190	    UNGROUNDED; Judge an off-enum token (so the 5-enum guard raises). Configurable per fixture.
191	    """
192	
193	    def __init__(self, *, judge_raw: str, sentinel_raw: str):
194	        self._judge_raw = judge_raw
195	        self._sentinel_raw = sentinel_raw
196	
197	    def complete(self, request):
198	        from src.polaris_graph.roles.mirror_contract import CitationSpan
199	        from src.polaris_graph.roles.role_transport import RoleResponse
200	
201	        if request.role == "mirror":
202	            if "pass2_input" in request.params:
203	                content_hash = request.params["pass2_input"]["content_hash"]
204	                payload = {"content_hash": content_hash, "classification": "supported"}
205	                return RoleResponse(
206	                    raw_text=json.dumps(payload), served_model=request.model_slug
207	                )
208	            return RoleResponse(
209	                raw_text="grounded answer",
210	                served_model=request.model_slug,
211	                citations=[CitationSpan(span_start=0, span_end=8, doc_ids=("doc1",))],
212	            )
213	        if request.role == "sentinel":
214	            return RoleResponse(raw_text=self._sentinel_raw, served_model=request.model_slug)
215	        if request.role == "judge":
216	            return RoleResponse(raw_text=self._judge_raw, served_model=request.model_slug)
217	        raise AssertionError(f"unexpected role {request.role!r}")
218	
219	
220	def check_role_contracts() -> CheckResult:
221	    """Exercise the three role contracts THROUGH the mock RoleTransport + adapters (Codex P2).
222	
223	    Routes each fixture through the real adapter -> injected `_GateAMockTransport` -> contract
224	    path (NOT direct parser calls), so check (d) is the fixture-through-transport check the
225	    brief required. Asserts:
226	    - Sentinel ``<score>yes</score>`` => UNGROUNDED (lethal-polarity: yes=risk=ungrounded).
227	    - Judge off-enum token => JudgeEnumError (no silent default for the terminal arbiter).
228	    - Mirror two-pass grounded round trip returns a bound MirrorPass2 (binding held).
229	    No network, no spend: the transport is the in-process mock.
230	    """
231	    from src.polaris_graph.roles.judge_adapter import run_judge
232	    from src.polaris_graph.roles.judge_contract import JudgeEnumError
233	    from src.polaris_graph.roles.mirror_adapter import run_mirror
234	    from src.polaris_graph.roles.role_transport import EvidenceDocument
235	    from src.polaris_graph.roles.sentinel_adapter import run_sentinel
236	    from src.polaris_graph.roles.sentinel_contract import SentinelVerdict
237	
238	    transport = _GateAMockTransport(judge_raw=_JUDGE_OFF_ENUM, sentinel_raw=_SENTINEL_YES)
239	    # The mock Mirror pass-1 cites doc_ids=("doc1",); supply that as a real evidence document so
240	    # the grounding-integrity binding accepts it.
241	    evidence = [EvidenceDocument(doc_id="doc1", text="grounded answer source span")]
242	    claim = "The grounded answer."
243	    problems: list[str] = []
244	
245	    # Sentinel GUARDIAN polarity through the adapter+transport: yes => UNGROUNDED, parsed_ok True.
246	    # mode="guardian" is pinned so this lethal-polarity fixture is mode-deterministic regardless of
247	    # the global default (I-run11-004: the default is now the MiniMax-M2 decomposition mode).
248	    sentinel_result, _ = run_sentinel(
249	        transport, claim, evidence,
250	        model_slug="ibm-granite/granite-guardian-4.1-8b", mode="guardian",
251	    )
252	    if sentinel_result.verdict is not SentinelVerdict.UNGROUNDED or not sentinel_result.parsed_ok:
253	        problems.append(
254	            f"Sentinel polarity wrong via transport: {sentinel_result} (expected UNGROUNDED)"
255	        )
256	
257	    # Sentinel DECOMPOSITION contract (I-run11-004): the CERTIFIED MiniMax-M2 JSON verdict
258	    # "unsupported" => UNGROUNDED, "supported" => GROUNDED (mode pinned for determinism).
259	    decomp_unsupported = _GateAMockTransport(
260	        judge_raw=_JUDGE_OFF_ENUM,
261	        sentinel_raw='{"verdict": "unsupported", "unsupported_atoms": 1, "atoms": []}',
262	    )
263	    decomp_result, _ = run_sentinel(
264	        decomp_unsupported, claim, evidence,
265	        model_slug="minimax/minimax-m2", mode="decomposition",
266	    )
267	    if decomp_result.verdict is not SentinelVerdict.UNGROUNDED or not decomp_result.parsed_ok:
268	        problems.append(
269	            f"Sentinel decomposition unsupported wrong via transport: {decomp_result} "
270	            "(expected UNGROUNDED)"
271	        )
272	    decomp_supported = _GateAMockTransport(
273	        judge_raw=_JUDGE_OFF_ENUM,
274	        # Full decomposition contract (I-run11-004 brief-gate P1): a "supported" verdict needs a
275	        # non-empty atoms list + unsupported_atoms, else the parser fails closed (a bare/non-atomized
276	        # "supported" did no per-atom work and must not release).
277	        sentinel_raw='{"verdict": "supported", "unsupported_atoms": 0, "atoms": [{"atom": "x", "type": "mechanism", "status": "supported"}]}',
278	    )
279	    decomp_grounded, _ = run_sentinel(
280	        decomp_supported, claim, evidence,
281	        model_slug="minimax/minimax-m2", mode="decomposition",
282	    )
283	    if decomp_grounded.verdict is not SentinelVerdict.GROUNDED or not decomp_grounded.parsed_ok:
284	        problems.append(
285	            f"Sentinel decomposition supported wrong via transport: {decomp_grounded} "
286	            "(expected GROUNDED)"
287	        )
288	
289	    # Judge through the adapter+transport: an off-enum token must RAISE (no silent default).
290	    # I-judge-kimi (2026-06-29): the benchmark Judge is now moonshotai/kimi-k2.6 (was qwen); the
291	    # off-enum rejection under test is model-agnostic, so this just reflects the current Judge slug.
292	    try:
293	        run_judge(
294	            transport,
295	            claim,
296	            evidence,
297	            "supported",
298	            "ungrounded",
299	            model_slug="moonshotai/kimi-k2.6",
300	        )
301	        problems.append(
302	            f"Judge accepted off-enum token {_JUDGE_OFF_ENUM!r} via transport "
303	            "(should raise JudgeEnumError)"
304	        )
305	    except JudgeEnumError:
306	        pass
307	
308	    # Mirror through the adapter+transport: the two-pass grounded round trip binds and returns.
309	    try:
310	        mirror_pass2, mirror_records = run_mirror(
311	            transport, claim, evidence, model_slug="z-ai/glm-5.1"
312	        )
313	        if mirror_pass2 is None or len(mirror_records) != 2:
314	            problems.append(
315	                f"Mirror two-pass via transport did not return a bound pass-2 + 2 records "
316	                f"(got pass2={mirror_pass2!r}, records={len(mirror_records)})"
317	            )
318	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 - surface any fail-closed raise as a contract problem
319	        problems.append(f"Mirror two-pass via transport raised unexpectedly: {exc!r}")
320	
321	    if problems:
322	        return CheckResult("role_contracts", False, "; ".join(problems))
323	    return CheckResult(
324	        "role_contracts",
325	        True,
326	        "via transport: Sentinel guardian yes=UNGROUNDED + decomposition "
327	        "supported=GROUNDED/unsupported=UNGROUNDED, Judge off-enum raises, Mirror two-pass binds",
328	    )
329	
330	
331	# --- cheap live probes (default OFF; ADVISORY ONLY; never part of PASS) --------------------
332	def run_live_probes() -> list[CheckResult]:
333	    """Run the 3 cheap real probes (Serper / Semantic Scholar / DeepSeek) — ADVISORY ONLY.
334	
335	    Gated behind ``--with-live-probes``; NEVER part of the PASS criteria (the dry run is
336	    no-spend by default). Each probe is best-effort: a failure is reported as advisory, not a
337	    gate failure. Implemented as a documented stub here — the live probe wiring is intentionally
338	    minimal so the dry-run default path stays zero-network.
339	    """
340	    return [
341	        CheckResult(
342	            "live_probe_serper",
343	            False,
344	            "advisory stub: live Serper probe not wired in the no-spend harness",
345	        ),
346	        CheckResult(
347	            "live_probe_semantic_scholar",
348	            False,
349	            "advisory stub: live Semantic Scholar probe not wired in the no-spend harness",
350	        ),
351	        CheckResult(
352	            "live_probe_deepseek",
353	            False,
354	            "advisory stub: live DeepSeek probe not wired in the no-spend harness",
355	        ),
356	    ]
357	
358	
359	def run_gate_a(*, with_live_probes: bool = False) -> GateAReport:
360	    """Run all four no-spend Gate-A checks. PASS iff (a) AND (b) AND (c) AND (d) are green.
361	
362	    The cheap live probes are run ONLY when ``with_live_probes`` is True and are ADVISORY —
363	    they never affect ``overall_pass``.
364	    """
365	    checks = [
366	        check_pytest_suites(),
367	        check_lock_consistency(),
368	        check_frozen_lock_coverage(),
369	        check_role_contracts(),
370	    ]
371	    overall = all(c.passed for c in checks)
372	    report = GateAReport(overall_pass=overall, checks=checks)
373	    if with_live_probes:
374	        report.live_probes_ran = True
375	        report.live_probe_results = run_live_probes()
376	    return report
377	
378	
379	def _render_human_summary(report: GateAReport) -> str:
380	    lines = [
381	        "POLARIS Gate-A no-spend dry run",
382	        f"  OVERALL: {'PASS' if report.overall_pass else 'FAIL'} (no-spend, offline)",
383	        "",
384	    ]
385	    for check in report.checks:
386	        lines.append(f"  [{'PASS' if check.passed else 'FAIL'}] {check.name}: {check.detail}")
387	    if report.live_probes_ran:
388	        lines.append("")
389	        lines.append("  Live probes (ADVISORY ONLY — not part of PASS):")
390	        for probe in report.live_probe_results:
391	            lines.append(
392	                f"    [{'ok' if probe.passed else 'advisory'}] {probe.name}: {probe.detail}"
393	            )
394	    lines.append("")
395	    lines.append(
396	        "  Gate-A PASS does NOT authorize spend. Lock promotion to status: locked is the "
397	        "operator's separate spend gate."
398	    )
399	    return "\n".join(lines)
400	
401	
402	def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
403	    """CLI entry: run Gate-A, write the JSON report + a human summary, return 0 iff PASS."""
404	    import argparse
405	
406	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="POLARIS Gate-A no-spend dry run.")
407	    parser.add_argument(
408	        "--with-live-probes",
409	        action="store_true",
410	        help=(
411	            "Run the 3 cheap real probes (Serper/S2/DeepSeek). ADVISORY ONLY — default OFF so "
412	            "the dry run is pure zero-spend. Never affects PASS/FAIL."
413	        ),
414	    )
415	    parser.add_argument(
416	        "--report-path",
417	        default=str(_DEFAULT_REPORT_PATH),
418	        help="Where to write the machine-readable JSON report.",
419	    )
420	    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
421	
422	    report = run_gate_a(with_live_probes=args.with_live_probes)
423	
424	    report_path = Path(args.report_path)
425	    report_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
426	    report_path.write_text(
427	        json.dumps(report.to_json_dict(), indent=2, sort_keys=True) + "\n",
428	        encoding="utf-8",
429	    )
430	    summary = _render_human_summary(report)
431	    print(summary)
432	    print(f"\nJSON report: {report_path}")
433	    return 0 if report.overall_pass else 1
434	
435	
436	if __name__ == "__main__":
437	    sys.exit(main())
438

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""WS-0 (I-deepfix-001 #1344) — the run-launch GPU device-split config + a co-residence preflight.
3	
4	WHY THIS EXISTS (the drb_72 GPU-OOM degrade cascade)
5	----------------------------------------------------
6	On the 2-card VM (2x RTX3090Ti, ~24GB each) the paid Gate-B run put the W5 content-relevance reranker,
7	the W6 Qwen3-Embedding-8B embedder, and the W10 consolidation NLI cross-encoder ALL on ``cuda:0`` (every
8	device env unset defaults the model to card 0). The 16GB embedder + the reranker's ~24.8GB one-pass
9	logits tensor then OOM'd, and the run SILENTLY DEGRADED: W2 semantic-relevance fell back to lexical,
10	consolidation fell to the CPU wall (under-merge), and GLM credibility-tiering fell to the rules-floor.
11	That single infra fault is the upstream root of the thin single-origin corpus (B2 Recall + Analysis).
12	
13	THE FIX (proven 2026-06-30) — a 2-card device split + a bounded W5 score chunk
14	------------------------------------------------------------------------------
15	  * ``PG_EMBED_DEVICE=cuda:0``            — W6 Qwen3-Embedding-8B alone on card 0 (~16GB fp16)
16	  * ``PG_RERANKER_DEVICE=cuda:1``         — W7 Qwen3-Reranker-4B on card 1
17	  * ``PG_NLI_DEVICE=cuda:1``              — FaithLens NLI cross-encoder on card 1 (off the critical path)
18	  * ``PG_CONSOLIDATION_NLI_DEVICE=cuda:1``— W10 consolidation NLI cross-encoder on card 1
19	  * ``PG_CONTENT_RELEVANCE_SCORE_CHUNK=2``— W5 reranker scores in chunks so the per-forward logits tensor
20	    stays small enough to fit alongside the co-resident embedder (faithfulness-neutral — chunked scores
21	    are byte-identical to the one-pass path; content_relevance_judge.py:442-455).
22	
23	The W5 content-relevance reranker (Qwen3-Reranker-0.6B) stays on ``cuda:0`` (its own
24	``PG_CONTENT_RELEVANCE_DEVICE`` default "cuda") but is BOUNDED by the score chunk, so the split leaves
25	card 0 = big embedder + bounded 0.6B reranker, card 1 = 4B reranker + two small NLI cross-encoders.
26	
27	SINGLE-CARD HOSTS (e.g. an A100-80GB)
28	-------------------------------------
29	On a host with only one visible CUDA device, a split cannot help — everything co-resides, and on an
30	80GB card it fits with headroom. The card-split warning is therefore suppressed when ``device_count < 2``.
31	The W5 score-chunk bound STILL applies (it is cheap insurance against the 24.8GB one-pass tensor even on
32	a big card), so that warning is card-count-independent.
33	
34	BUILD-ONLY / NO SPEND / NO MODEL LOAD
35	-------------------------------------
36	Pure config. ``detect_coresidence_warnings`` is a pure function over an env map + a device count (offline-
37	testable, no GPU needed). Only ``--check`` touches the GPU — a free ``torch.cuda.device_count()`` read
38	(no model load, no completion call). ``--export`` emits sourceable ``export K=V`` lines for the launcher.
39	
40	LAW VI: the recommended values ARE this file's config surface (a documented launch template), not magic
41	numbers scattered through the run path. The run path READS these envs; this helper DECLARES them.
42	
43	Usage
44	-----
45	    # print the documented launch template (human-readable)
46	    python scripts/dr_benchmark/gpu_device_split.py
47	
48	    # emit sourceable export lines for a 2-card launch
49	    source <(python scripts/dr_benchmark/gpu_device_split.py --export)
50	    python scripts/dr_benchmark/run_gate_b.py --only <slug>
51	
52	    # preflight the CURRENT env against the visible GPU topology (warns on co-residence)
53	    python scripts/dr_benchmark/gpu_device_split.py --check     # exit 0 clean, 3 == warnings emitted
54	"""
55	
56	from __future__ import annotations
57	
58	import argparse
59	import os
60	import sys
61	from typing import Mapping, Optional
62	
63	# The recommended 2-card split + the W5 score-chunk bound (the proven 2026-06-30 fix). This is the
64	# documented launch template; the run path reads each key at model-load / call time.
65	RECOMMENDED_DEVICE_SPLIT: dict[str, str] = {
66	    "PG_EMBED_DEVICE": "cuda:0",              # W6 Qwen3-Embedding-8B (embedding_service:150 / prefetch_offtopic_filter:140)
67	    "PG_RERANKER_DEVICE": "cuda:1",           # W7 Qwen3-Reranker-4B (evidence_selector:2658 / qwen_reranker_scorer:83)
68	    "PG_NLI_DEVICE": "cuda:1",                # FaithLens NLI (nli_verifier:95, off the critical path)
69	    "PG_CONSOLIDATION_NLI_DEVICE": "cuda:1",  # W10 consolidation NLI cross-encoder (consolidation_nli:73)
70	    "PG_CONTENT_RELEVANCE_SCORE_CHUNK": "2",  # W5 reranker chunked scoring (content_relevance_judge:451)
71	}
72	
73	# The four HEAVY-model device envs whose co-residence on one card is the OOM-degrade risk. (The W5
74	# content-relevance reranker uses PG_CONTENT_RELEVANCE_DEVICE and is bounded by the score chunk, so it
75	# is intentionally NOT in the card-balance set — the score-chunk warning covers it.)
76	_HEAVY_MODEL_DEVICE_ENVS: dict[str, str] = {
77	    "PG_EMBED_DEVICE": "W6 embedder (Qwen3-Embedding-8B, ~16GB fp16)",
78	    "PG_RERANKER_DEVICE": "W7 reranker (Qwen3-Reranker-4B, ~8GB)",
79	    "PG_NLI_DEVICE": "FaithLens NLI cross-encoder",
80	    "PG_CONSOLIDATION_NLI_DEVICE": "W10 consolidation NLI cross-encoder",
81	}
82	
83	# An UNSET device env lands the model on the module default card (cuda:0). That default IS the OOM trap
84	# the split fixes, so an unset env resolves to card 0 for the co-residence check (never "unknown").
85	_DEFAULT_GPU_CARD = 0
86	
87	_SCORE_CHUNK_ENV = "PG_CONTENT_RELEVANCE_SCORE_CHUNK"
88	
89	
90	def resolve_gpu_card(value: Optional[str]) -> Optional[int]:
91	    """The GPU card index a device string targets. ``cuda:N`` -> N; ``cuda`` / bare ``N`` -> that index;
92	    UNSET/empty -> the module default card 0 (the co-residence trap); ``cpu`` or an unparseable value ->
93	    None (not a GPU card, excluded from the balance check)."""
94	    v = (value or "").strip().lower()
95	    if not v:
96	        return _DEFAULT_GPU_CARD
97	    if v == "cpu":
98	        return None
99	    if v == "cuda":
100	        return 0
101	    if v.startswith("cuda:"):
102	        tail = v.split(":", 1)[1]
103	        return int(tail) if tail.isdigit() else None
104	    if v.isdigit():
105	        return int(v)
106	    return None
107	
108	
109	def detect_coresidence_warnings(
110	    env: Mapping[str, str], device_count: Optional[int]
111	) -> list[str]:
112	    """Pure preflight. Return the list of WARNING strings for the given env + visible GPU count.
113	
114	    (1) CARD CO-RESIDENCE — only when ``device_count >= 2``: if every heavy-model device env resolves to
115	        a SINGLE GPU card, the models will co-reside and risk the GPU-OOM degrade cascade; recommend the
116	        split. Suppressed on a 1-GPU host (a split cannot help there).
117	    (2) W5 SCORE-CHUNK — card-count-independent: if ``PG_CONTENT_RELEVANCE_SCORE_CHUNK`` is unset/0/negative
118	        (one-pass), the ~24.8GB one-pass logits tensor can OOM the co-resident embedder; recommend =2.
119	    """
120	    warns: list[str] = []
121	
122	    if device_count is not None and device_count >= 2:
123	        gpu_cards: set[int] = set()
124	        for name in _HEAVY_MODEL_DEVICE_ENVS:
125	            card = resolve_gpu_card(env.get(name))
126	            if card is not None:
127	                gpu_cards.add(card)
128	        if len(gpu_cards) <= 1:
129	            only = next(iter(gpu_cards), _DEFAULT_GPU_CARD)
130	            split = ", ".join(
131	                f"{k}={RECOMMENDED_DEVICE_SPLIT[k]}"
132	                for k in _HEAVY_MODEL_DEVICE_ENVS
133	                if k in RECOMMENDED_DEVICE_SPLIT
134	            )
135	            warns.append(
136	                f"[gpu-device-split WARNING] {device_count} CUDA devices visible but every heavy model "
137	                f"resolves to a SINGLE card cuda:{only} "
138	                f"({', '.join(_HEAVY_MODEL_DEVICE_ENVS)}) — they will co-reside and risk a GPU-OOM degrade "
139	                f"(W5 reranker + W6 embedder + W10 NLI on one card was the drb_72 root). Apply the 2-card "
140	                f"split: {split}."
141	            )
142	
143	    try:
144	        chunk = int((env.get(_SCORE_CHUNK_ENV) or "0").strip())
145	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
146	        chunk = 0
147	    if chunk <= 0:
148	        warns.append(
149	            f"[gpu-device-split WARNING] {_SCORE_CHUNK_ENV} is unset/0 (one-pass) — the W5 Qwen3-Reranker "
150	            f"scores ALL pairs in one forward pass (~24.8GB logits tensor) and can OOM the co-resident "
151	            f"embedder on cuda:0. Set {_SCORE_CHUNK_ENV}=2 (faithfulness-neutral: chunked scores are "
152	            f"byte-identical to one-pass)."
153	        )
154	    return warns
155	
156	
157	def _visible_cuda_device_count() -> Optional[int]:
158	    """A FREE ``torch.cuda.device_count()`` read (no model load, no spend). None if torch is unavailable."""
159	    try:
160	        import torch  # noqa: PLC0415
161	    except ImportError:
162	        return None
163	    try:
164	        return int(torch.cuda.device_count())
165	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — a driver hiccup must never crash a preflight
166	        return None
167	
168	
169	def render_launch_template(as_export: bool = False) -> str:
170	    """The documented launch config. ``as_export=True`` -> sourceable ``export K=V`` lines; else a
171	    human-readable ``K=V  # why`` template."""
172	    lines: list[str] = []
173	    for key, value in RECOMMENDED_DEVICE_SPLIT.items():
174	        if as_export:
175	            lines.append(f"export {key}={value}")
176	        else:
177	            why = _HEAVY_MODEL_DEVICE_ENVS.get(key, "W5 reranker chunked scoring (one-pass OOM guard)")
178	            lines.append(f"{key}={value}  # {why}")
179	    return "\n".join(lines)
180	
181	
182	def main(argv: Optional[list[str]] = None) -> int:
183	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="WS-0 GPU device-split launch config + co-residence preflight.")
184	    ap.add_argument("--export", action="store_true", help="emit sourceable `export K=V` lines")
185	    ap.add_argument("--check", action="store_true",
186	                    help="preflight the CURRENT env vs the visible GPU topology (exit 3 if warnings)")
187	    args = ap.parse_args(argv)
188	
189	    if args.export:
190	        print(render_launch_template(as_export=True))
191	        return 0
192	
193	    if args.check:
194	        device_count = _visible_cuda_device_count()
195	        dc_note = "torch unavailable (card-split check skipped)" if device_count is None else f"{device_count} CUDA device(s)"
196	        print(f"[gpu-device-split] visible: {dc_note}")
197	        warns = detect_coresidence_warnings(os.environ, device_count)
198	        for w in warns:
199	            print(w)
200	        if not warns:
201	            print("[gpu-device-split] OK — no co-residence / one-pass OOM risk in the current env.")
202	        return 3 if warns else 0
203	
204	    # default: print the documented template.
205	    print("# WS-0 recommended 2-card GPU device split (source via --export):")
206	    print(render_launch_template(as_export=False))
207	    return 0
208	
209	
210	if __name__ == "__main__":
211	    sys.exit(main())
212

1	"""DICED harness — I-deepfix-001 Wave-2 (#1370): compose-stage ANTI-GLUE.
2	
3	Proves ``PG_COMPOSE_NO_UNRELATED_GLUE`` on FROZEN box2 artifacts (read-only), seconds-level, NO fresh
4	run / NO fetch / NO GPU / NO LLM. It exercises the SMALLEST callable join unit
5	``cross_source_synthesis._join_analytical_pair`` — the exact function the composer uses to join two
6	verified single-source clauses. It builds THREE clauses as verbatim substrings of REAL box2 evidence
7	spans (so each clause is faithful-by-construction and carries a real ``[#ev:<id>:<start>-<end>]`` token):
8	
9	  * clause_a          — ev_1226  "total publications using a Gaussian distribution"
10	  * clause_b_unrel    — ev_446   "was summed up by Informant 17"   (topically UNRELATED to clause_a)
11	  * clause_a2         — frey_osborne_computerisation  "the probability of computerisation for 702 detailed occupations"
12	  * clause_b_rel      — ev_165   "600 detailed occupations in over 300 occupational profiles"  (RELATED: shares "detailed","occupations")
13	
14	Signal:
15	  RED  (flag OFF): the topically-UNRELATED NEUTRAL pair is GLUED with "; separately," (incoherent run-on).
16	  GREEN(flag ON) : the UNRELATED pair renders as TWO SEPARATE sentences (no "; separately,"), while the
17	                   genuinely-RELATED pair STILL joins with "; separately," (the split is targeted).
18	
19	Run: PYTHONIOENCODING=utf-8 PYTHONPATH=<cwd> python scripts/harness_compose_no_unrelated_glue.py
20	"""
21	from __future__ import annotations
22	
23	import json
24	import os
25	import re
26	import sys
27	
28	SCRATCHPAD = (
29	    r"C:\Users\msn\AppData\Local\Temp\claude\C--POLARIS"
30	    r"\dde5b4ec-b98b-4784-a4d2-3b7fd5d3e391\scratchpad"
31	)
32	EVIDENCE_POOL = os.path.join(SCRATCHPAD, "box2_evidence_pool.json")
33	
34	# (evidence_id, verbatim_substring) — offsets are computed against the FROZEN pool so the token is honest.
35	CLAUSE_A = ("ev_1226", "total publications using a Gaussian distribution")
36	CLAUSE_B_UNRELATED = ("ev_446", "was summed up by Informant 17")
37	CLAUSE_A2 = ("frey_osborne_computerisation", "the probability of computerisation for 702 detailed occupations")
38	CLAUSE_B_RELATED = ("ev_165", "600 detailed occupations in over 300 occupational profiles")
39	
40	
41	def _load_pool() -> dict:
42	    with open(EVIDENCE_POOL, encoding="utf-8") as fh:
43	        rows = json.load(fh)
44	    return {r.get("evidence_id"): (r.get("direct_quote") or "") for r in rows}
45	
46	
47	def _build_clause(pool: dict, eid: str, substring: str) -> str:
48	    """Build a strict_verify-shaped clause: '<verbatim substring> [#ev:<id>:<start>-<end>]', where the
49	    span offsets are the REAL character range of the verbatim substring inside box2's direct_quote."""
50	    quote = pool.get(eid, "")
51	    start = quote.find(substring)
52	    if start < 0:
53	        raise SystemExit(f"BLOCKED: substring not found verbatim in {eid}: {substring!r}")
54	    end = start + len(substring)
55	    # Capitalize the leading char so the clause reads as a sentence unit (as split_into_sentences yields).
56	    prose = substring[0].upper() + substring[1:]
57	    return f"{prose} [#ev:{eid}:{start}-{end}]"
58	
59	
60	def main() -> int:
61	    from src.polaris_graph.generator import cross_source_synthesis as css
62	
63	    pool = _load_pool()
64	    clause_a = _build_clause(pool, *CLAUSE_A)
65	    clause_b_unrel = _build_clause(pool, *CLAUSE_B_UNRELATED)
66	    clause_a2 = _build_clause(pool, *CLAUSE_A2)
67	    clause_b_rel = _build_clause(pool, *CLAUSE_B_RELATED)
68	
69	    ov_unrel = len(css._glue_content_words(clause_a) & css._glue_content_words(clause_b_unrel))
70	    ov_rel = len(css._glue_content_words(clause_a2) & css._glue_content_words(clause_b_rel))
71	
72	    print("=" * 78)
73	    print("DICED HARNESS — PG_COMPOSE_NO_UNRELATED_GLUE (compose-stage anti-glue)")
74	    print("=" * 78)
75	    print(f"clause_a         : {clause_a}")
76	    print(f"clause_b_unrel   : {clause_b_unrel}")
77	    print(f"  content-word overlap(unrelated) = {ov_unrel}")
78	    print(f"clause_a2        : {clause_a2}")
79	    print(f"clause_b_rel     : {clause_b_rel}")
80	    print(f"  content-word overlap(related)   = {ov_rel}")
81	    print("-" * 78)
82	
83	    glue = "; separately,"
84	
85	    def _glued(text: str) -> bool:
86	        return glue in text
87	
88	    def _two_sentences(text: str) -> bool:
89	        # A period/terminal followed by whitespace + capital = a real sentence boundary (the production
90	        # _SENT_SPLIT_RE shape: terminal .!?] + whitespace + [A-Z0-9]).
91	        return bool(re.search(r"[.!?\]]\s+[A-Z0-9]", text))
92	
93	    # ---- RED: flag OFF (default) — the UNRELATED neutral pair is GLUED ----
94	    os.environ.pop("PG_COMPOSE_NO_UNRELATED_GLUE", None)
95	    assert not css.compose_no_unrelated_glue_enabled(), "flag should default OFF"
96	    off_unrel = css._join_analytical_pair(clause_a, clause_b_unrel, "neutral")
97	    off_rel = css._join_analytical_pair(clause_a2, clause_b_rel, "neutral")
98	    print("[flag OFF]")
99	    print(f"  unrelated -> {off_unrel!r}")
100	    print(f"  related   -> {off_rel!r}")
101	
102	    red_ok = _glued(off_unrel) and _glued(off_rel)
103	    print(f"  RED expectation (both glued with '; separately,') : {'RED CONFIRMED' if red_ok else 'NOT MET'}")
104	    print("-" * 78)
105	
106	    # ---- GREEN: flag ON — the UNRELATED pair SPLITS; the RELATED pair stays glued ----
107	    os.environ["PG_COMPOSE_NO_UNRELATED_GLUE"] = "1"
108	    assert css.compose_no_unrelated_glue_enabled(), "flag should be ON"
109	    on_unrel = css._join_analytical_pair(clause_a, clause_b_unrel, "neutral")
110	    on_rel = css._join_analytical_pair(clause_a2, clause_b_rel, "neutral")
111	    print("[flag ON]")
112	    print(f"  unrelated -> {on_unrel!r}")
113	    print(f"  related   -> {on_rel!r}")
114	
115	    # Faithfulness-neutral check: same two evidence_ids survive the split.
116	    ids_off = css._distinct_ev_ids(off_unrel)
117	    ids_on = css._distinct_ev_ids(on_unrel)
118	
119	    green_unrel_split = (not _glued(on_unrel)) and _two_sentences(on_unrel)
120	    green_rel_kept = _glued(on_rel)
121	    green_ids_preserved = ids_on == ids_off and len(ids_on) == 2
122	    green_ok = green_unrel_split and green_rel_kept and green_ids_preserved
123	
124	    print(f"  unrelated split into 2 sentences, no '; separately,' : {green_unrel_split}")
125	    print(f"  related STILL glued with '; separately,'             : {green_rel_kept}")
126	    print(f"  citations preserved (same 2 ev-ids OFF vs ON)        : {green_ids_preserved} {sorted(ids_on)}")
127	    print("=" * 78)
128	
129	    if red_ok and green_ok:
130	        print("RESULT: GREEN — fix splits topically-unrelated neutral glue, keeps related pairs joined,")
131	        print("        preserves both citations; OFF is byte-identical to current behaviour.")
132	        return 0
133	    print("RESULT: FAIL — see expectations above.")
134	    return 1
135	
136	
137	if __name__ == "__main__":
138	    sys.exit(main())

1	"""Path-B dual-§-1.1-audit ledger schema (I-safety-002b #925 PR-3).
2	
3	The per-(system × question) JSON each auditor (Claude / Codex) writes for the dual line-by-line
4	audit. Mirrors `src.polaris_graph.benchmark.claim_audit_scorer.ClaimRow` validation so a ledger
5	that fails-closed here cannot smuggle a malformed row into the scorer. Pure stdlib + dataclasses
6	(no pydantic dep added).
7	
8	Public surface:
9	- ``Claim``       — one atomic claim audited against the FETCHED cited span.
10	- ``Coverage``    — one pre-registered gold-rubric element's coverage by THIS system's report.
11	- ``Ledger``      — the full (system, question) audit ledger written by ONE auditor.
12	- ``load_ledger`` — read+validate a ledger JSON from disk.
13	- ``dump_ledger`` — write a validated ledger JSON to disk.
14	"""
15	
16	from __future__ import annotations
17	
18	import json
19	from dataclasses import asdict, dataclass, field
20	from pathlib import Path
21	from typing import Literal
22	
23	# Mirrored from claim_audit_scorer.py (PR-1) — do NOT diverge.
24	Verdict = Literal["VERIFIED", "PARTIAL", "UNSUPPORTED", "FABRICATED", "UNREACHABLE"]
25	Severity = Literal["S0", "S1", "S2", "S3"]
26	UnreachableSubtype = Literal["paywall", "robots", "fetch_failure", "source_missing"]
27	Auditor = Literal["claude", "codex", "reconciled"]
28	SystemId = Literal["polaris", "chatgpt", "gemini"]
29	
30	_VERDICTS = ("VERIFIED", "PARTIAL", "UNSUPPORTED", "FABRICATED", "UNREACHABLE")
31	_SEVERITIES = ("S0", "S1", "S2", "S3")
32	_UNREACHABLE_SUBTYPES = ("paywall", "robots", "fetch_failure", "source_missing")
33	# "reconciled" is the output of dual-auditor reconciliation (PR-3 reconcile.py), NOT a
34	# real auditor — the upstream auditors are recorded in audit_method.
35	_AUDITORS = ("claude", "codex", "reconciled")
36	_SYSTEMS = ("polaris", "chatgpt", "gemini")
37	
38	
39	@dataclass
40	class Claim:
41	    """One atomic claim's audit row. Validation MIRRORS ClaimRow.__post_init__:
42	    UNREACHABLE requires unreachable_subtype; FABRICATED/PARTIAL require span_quote;
43	    UNSUPPORTED+cited requires span_quote OR audit_note (traceability)."""
44	
45	    claim_id: str
46	    severity: str            # "S0"|"S1"|"S2"|"S3"
47	    verdict: str             # one of _VERDICTS
48	    citation_id: str | None = None
49	    span_quote: str | None = None
50	    unreachable_subtype: str | None = None
51	    audit_note: str | None = None
52	
53	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
54	        if not self.claim_id:
55	            raise ValueError("Claim.claim_id is required")
56	        if self.severity not in _SEVERITIES:
57	            raise ValueError(f"{self.claim_id}: severity {self.severity!r} not in {_SEVERITIES}")
58	        if self.verdict not in _VERDICTS:
59	            raise ValueError(f"{self.claim_id}: verdict {self.verdict!r} not in {_VERDICTS}")
60	        if self.verdict == "UNREACHABLE" and self.unreachable_subtype is None:
61	            raise ValueError(f"{self.claim_id}: UNREACHABLE requires unreachable_subtype")
62	        if self.verdict != "UNREACHABLE" and self.unreachable_subtype is not None:
63	            raise ValueError(
64	                f"{self.claim_id}: unreachable_subtype only valid for UNREACHABLE"
65	            )
66	        if (
67	            self.unreachable_subtype is not None
68	            and self.unreachable_subtype not in _UNREACHABLE_SUBTYPES
69	        ):
70	            raise ValueError(
71	                f"{self.claim_id}: unreachable_subtype {self.unreachable_subtype!r} "
72	                f"not in {_UNREACHABLE_SUBTYPES}"
73	            )
74	        if self.verdict in ("FABRICATED", "PARTIAL") and not self.span_quote:
75	            raise ValueError(
76	                f"{self.claim_id}: {self.verdict} requires a span_quote (the refuting/partial span)"
77	            )
78	        if (
79	            self.verdict == "UNSUPPORTED"
80	            and self.citation_id
81	            and not (self.span_quote or self.audit_note)
82	        ):
83	            raise ValueError(
84	                f"{self.claim_id}: UNSUPPORTED+cited requires a span_quote or audit_note (traceability)"
85	            )
86	
87	
88	@dataclass
89	class Coverage:
90	    """One pre-registered gold-rubric element's coverage row, produced in the SAME
91	    pass as the per-claim audit (Codex PR-3 design answer B)."""
92	
93	    element_id: str
94	    covered: bool
95	    citation_supported: bool
96	    auditor_note: str | None = None
97	
98	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
99	        if not self.element_id:
100	            raise ValueError("Coverage.element_id is required")
101	        # Codex PR-3 diff P2 #1: enforce real bools. JSON deserialization can yield strings
102	        # ("false", "0", "") that are truthy in Python and would silently pass scoring.
103	        for fname in ("covered", "citation_supported"):
104	            v = getattr(self, fname)
105	            if not isinstance(v, bool):
106	                raise ValueError(
107	                    f"Coverage.{fname} must be a bool, got {type(v).__name__}: {v!r}"
108	                )
109	
110	
111	@dataclass
112	class Ledger:
113	    """The full (system, question) audit ledger written by ONE auditor (claude OR codex)."""
114	
115	    system: str           # one of _SYSTEMS
116	    question_id: str      # "Q72"|"Q75"|"Q76"|"Q78"|"Q90"
117	    auditor: str          # one of _AUDITORS
118	    audit_method: str     # short description, e.g. "dual-§-1.1-line-by-line-2026-05-28"
119	    audit_timestamp_utc: str
120	    rubric_sha256: str    # the freeze_pin.txt SHA of the rubric being audited against
121	    claims: list[Claim] = field(default_factory=list)
122	    coverage: list[Coverage] = field(default_factory=list)
123	
124	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
125	        if self.system not in _SYSTEMS:
126	            raise ValueError(f"system {self.system!r} not in {_SYSTEMS}")
127	        if self.auditor not in _AUDITORS:
128	            raise ValueError(f"auditor {self.auditor!r} not in {_AUDITORS}")
129	        if not self.question_id:
130	            raise ValueError("Ledger.question_id is required")
131	        if not self.audit_method:
132	            raise ValueError("Ledger.audit_method is required (audit-protocol identity)")
133	        if not self.audit_timestamp_utc:
134	            raise ValueError("Ledger.audit_timestamp_utc is required (audit-time identity)")
135	        if not self.rubric_sha256:
136	            raise ValueError("Ledger.rubric_sha256 is required (pre-registration anchor)")
137	        # Duplicate detection: claim_id and element_id are unique within a ledger.
138	        seen_claims = set()
139	        for c in self.claims:
140	            if c.claim_id in seen_claims:
141	                raise ValueError(f"duplicate claim_id {c.claim_id!r}")
142	            seen_claims.add(c.claim_id)
143	        seen_elements = set()
144	        for cv in self.coverage:
145	            if cv.element_id in seen_elements:
146	                raise ValueError(f"duplicate element_id {cv.element_id!r}")
147	            seen_elements.add(cv.element_id)
148	
149	
150	def load_ledger(path: Path) -> Ledger:
151	    """Read a ledger JSON; validate per dataclass __post_init__; raise on any malformed row."""
152	    raw = json.loads(Path(path).read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
153	    claims = [Claim(**c) for c in raw.get("claims", [])]
154	    coverage = [Coverage(**c) for c in raw.get("coverage", [])]
155	    head = {k: v for k, v in raw.items() if k not in ("claims", "coverage")}
156	    return Ledger(claims=claims, coverage=coverage, **head)
157	
158	
159	def dump_ledger(ledger: Ledger, path: Path) -> None:
160	    """Write a ledger JSON (validated by construction; no extra validation needed)."""
161	    out = asdict(ledger)
162	    Path(path).parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
163	    Path(path).write_text(
164	        json.dumps(out, indent=2, sort_keys=True, default=str) + "\n",
165	        encoding="utf-8",
166	    )
167

1	"""Pure scoring/adapter logic for the POLARIS-vs-MedHallu verification-layer benchmark.
2	
3	I-safety-002a (#924). Codex-designed run (see .codex/I-safety-002a/codex_medhallu_design.txt):
4	map MedHallu onto POLARIS's ENTAILMENT-verification layer (NOT strict_verify, which
5	needs provenance tokens and cannot run on raw MedHallu answers). Per row -> two
6	candidates (Ground Truth = faithful, Hallucinated = hallucinated); source = Question +
7	Knowledge ONLY (never the candidate answers); atomize the answer; verify each claim by
8	entailment against the Knowledge; aggregate: answer is hallucinated iff ANY substantive
9	claim is unsupported. Positive class = hallucinated.
10	
11	This module holds ONLY pure functions (no model, no I/O) so the scorer can be validated
12	on fixtures BEFORE any model run, per the Codex-APPROVE'd plan.
13	"""
14	
15	from __future__ import annotations
16	
17	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
18	
19	# Codex: pin the shipped direct-verifier threshold; the headline uses the verifier's
20	# internal threshold via PG_FAITHFULNESS_NLI_THRESHOLD. This mirror locks the boundary.
21	DEFAULT_NLI_FAITH_THRESHOLD = 0.65
22	
23	
24	class StrictVerifyMisuseError(RuntimeError):
25	    """Raised if anything tries to score raw MedHallu answers with strict_verify.
26	
27	    strict_verify requires POLARIS provenance tokens ([#ev:id:start-end]); raw MedHallu
28	    answers have none, so strict_verify would return no_provenance_token for every row and
29	    produce a junk 0%-specificity "everything is hallucinated" result (Codex ruling).
30	    """
31	
32	
33	@dataclass
34	class Candidate:
35	    """One scored unit: a candidate answer with its mechanical gold label."""
36	
37	    row_id: str
38	    split: str
39	    answer_text: str
40	    gold_hallucinated: bool
41	    candidate_kind: str  # "ground_truth" | "hallucinated"
42	
43	
44	def pair_row(row: dict, split: str) -> list[Candidate]:
45	    """Row -> exactly two candidates: Ground Truth (faithful) + Hallucinated (positive).
46	
47	    Fixture 1 (pairing) + the per-row clustering unit for bootstrap CIs.
48	    """
49	    row_id = str(row["row_id"])
50	    gt = (row.get("ground_truth") or "").strip()
51	    hallu = (row.get("hallucinated_answer") or "").strip()
52	    return [
53	        Candidate(row_id, split, gt, gold_hallucinated=False, candidate_kind="ground_truth"),
54	        Candidate(row_id, split, hallu, gold_hallucinated=True, candidate_kind="hallucinated"),
55	    ]
56	
57	
58	def build_source_text(question: str, knowledge: str) -> str:
59	    """Verifier source = Question + Knowledge ONLY.
60	
61	    Codex: the candidate answer (esp. Ground Truth) MUST NOT enter the evidence, or the
62	    negative label leaks. Fixture 2 (source isolation) asserts no candidate text leaks here.
63	    """
64	    return f"Question: {question.strip()}\n\nKnowledge: {knowledge.strip()}"
65	
66	
67	def assert_source_isolated(source_text: str, candidate: Candidate) -> None:
68	    """Fail-closed if a candidate answer leaked into the verifier source (Codex fixture 2)."""
69	    ans = candidate.answer_text.strip()
70	    if ans and ans in source_text:
71	        raise ValueError(
72	            f"label leak: candidate ({candidate.candidate_kind}) answer text found in "
73	            f"verifier source for row {candidate.row_id}"
74	        )
75	
76	
77	def build_evidence_object(claim: str, question: str, knowledge: str, candidate: Candidate) -> dict:
78	    """Build the single evidence dict fed to verify_evidence_nli (Codex spec).
79	
80	    source content = Question + Knowledge; statement = the claim under test. Guards source
81	    isolation. research_query is left "" by the caller to disable off-topic keyword overrides.
82	    """
83	    source_text = build_source_text(question, knowledge)
84	    assert_source_isolated(source_text, candidate)
85	    source_url = f"medhallu:{candidate.split}:{candidate.row_id}:{candidate.candidate_kind}"
86	    return {
87	        "statement": claim,
88	        "source_url": source_url,
89	        "source_title": question.strip(),
90	        "direct_quote": knowledge.strip(),
91	    }
92	
93	
94	def claim_is_faithful(prob: float, label: int, threshold: float = DEFAULT_NLI_FAITH_THRESHOLD) -> bool:
95	    """Mirror of the verifier's rule: faithful iff entailed (label==1) AND prob >= threshold.
96	
97	    Fixture 5 (threshold boundary): 0.65 passes with >=, 0.6499 fails.
98	    """
99	    return bool(label == 1) and prob >= threshold
100	
101	
102	def aggregate_answer_verdict(claim_faithful_flags: list[bool]) -> str:
103	    """Answer-level verdict from per-claim faithfulness (Codex aggregation rule).
104	
105	    - no substantive claims -> "invalid" (report separately; do not silently drop).
106	    - all claims faithful    -> "faithful".
107	    - any claim unsupported   -> "hallucinated".
108	    Fixture 4 (aggregation).
109	    """
110	    if not claim_faithful_flags:
111	        return "invalid"
112	    return "faithful" if all(claim_faithful_flags) else "hallucinated"
113	
114	
115	@dataclass
116	class Confusion:
117	    """Positive class = hallucinated."""
118	
119	    tp: int = 0  # predicted hallucinated, gold hallucinated
120	    fp: int = 0  # predicted hallucinated, gold faithful
121	    fn: int = 0  # predicted faithful, gold hallucinated
122	    tn: int = 0  # predicted faithful, gold faithful
123	    invalid: int = 0  # unparseable/empty answers (reported, not scored)
124	
125	
126	def add_prediction(conf: Confusion, predicted_verdict: str, gold_hallucinated: bool) -> None:
127	    """Fold one candidate prediction into the confusion matrix. Invalids counted aside."""
128	    if predicted_verdict == "invalid":
129	        conf.invalid += 1
130	        return
131	    pred_hallu = predicted_verdict == "hallucinated"
132	    if pred_hallu and gold_hallucinated:
133	        conf.tp += 1
134	    elif pred_hallu and not gold_hallucinated:
135	        conf.fp += 1
136	    elif (not pred_hallu) and gold_hallucinated:
137	        conf.fn += 1
138	    else:
139	        conf.tn += 1
140	
141	
142	def _safe_div(num: float, den: float) -> float:
143	    return num / den if den else 0.0
144	
145	
146	def metrics(conf: Confusion) -> dict:
147	    """F1/precision/recall/specificity/balanced-accuracy. Positive class = hallucinated.
148	
149	    Fixture 6 (confusion -> known F1).
150	    """
151	    precision = _safe_div(conf.tp, conf.tp + conf.fp)
152	    recall = _safe_div(conf.tp, conf.tp + conf.fn)  # = sensitivity
153	    specificity = _safe_div(conf.tn, conf.tn + conf.fp)
154	    f1 = _safe_div(2 * precision * recall, precision + recall)
155	    balanced_accuracy = (recall + specificity) / 2
156	    return {
157	        "f1": f1,
158	        "precision": precision,
159	        "recall": recall,
160	        "specificity": specificity,
161	        "balanced_accuracy": balanced_accuracy,
162	        "tp": conf.tp,
163	        "fp": conf.fp,
164	        "fn": conf.fn,
165	        "tn": conf.tn,
166	        "invalid": conf.invalid,
167	    }
168	
169	
170	def expected_candidate_count(n_labeled: int, n_artificial: int) -> dict:
171	    """Pairing-count math: each row -> 2 candidates (Codex fixture 7)."""
172	    return {
173	        "pqa_labeled": n_labeled * 2,
174	        "pqa_artificial": n_artificial * 2,
175	        "total": (n_labeled + n_artificial) * 2,
176	    }
177	
178	
179	@dataclass
180	class RunGuards:
181	    """Run-level guards that must hold for the headline number to be valid (Codex)."""
182	
183	    nli_model_available: bool = True
184	    llm_fallback_used: bool = False
185	    negative_control: bool = False
186	    notes: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
187	
188	    def assert_headline_valid(self) -> None:
189	        """Headline run aborts if the NLI model is unavailable (Codex fixture 8: no silent
190	        fallback) or if it is a negative-control run masquerading as the headline."""
191	        if not self.nli_model_available:
192	            raise RuntimeError(
193	                "NLI model unavailable: verify_evidence_nli returned [] — abort the headline "
194	                "run, do NOT silently fall back to an LLM (Codex contamination control)."
195	            )
196	        if self.negative_control:
197	            raise RuntimeError(
198	                "negative-control run cannot be recorded as the headline artifact "
199	                "(Codex fixture 9)."
200	            )
201

1	"""Run POLARIS's entailment-verification layer against MedHallu (I-safety-002a / #924).
2	
3	Codex-designed (.codex/I-safety-002a/codex_medhallu_design.txt). Honest scope: this measures
4	POLARIS's NLI/entailment verifier as a MedHallu detector — NOT the full provenance pipeline
5	(strict_verify cannot run on raw MedHallu answers). Positive class = hallucinated.
6	
7	Frozen knobs (set BEFORE importing the verifier): pinned NLI model, no cross-source, no
8	domain-adaptive, shipped threshold, NO LLM fallback for the headline (abort if NLI down).
9	
10	Usage:
11	  python -m scripts.dr_benchmark.medhallu_runner --split pqa_labeled --limit 10
12	  python -m scripts.dr_benchmark.medhallu_runner --split pqa_labeled --limit 10 --negative-control
13	"""
14	
15	from __future__ import annotations
16	
17	import argparse
18	import asyncio
19	import json
20	import os
21	import random
22	from datetime import datetime, timezone
23	from pathlib import Path
24	
25	# --- Codex: freeze verifier knobs BEFORE importing the NLI verifier (module reads at import) ---
26	os.environ.setdefault("PG_NLI_MODEL", "flan-t5-large")  # pinned, not "auto"
27	os.environ["PG_CROSS_SOURCE_ENABLED"] = "0"             # MedHallu = one gold packet, no corroboration
28	os.environ["PG_NLI_DOMAIN_ADAPTIVE"] = "0"
29	os.environ.setdefault("PG_FAITHFULNESS_NLI_THRESHOLD", "0.65")
30	
31	import pysbd  # noqa: E402
32	
33	from scripts.dr_benchmark.medhallu_adapter import (  # noqa: E402
34	    Confusion,
35	    RunGuards,
36	    add_prediction,
37	    aggregate_answer_verdict,
38	    build_evidence_object,
39	    expected_candidate_count,
40	    metrics,
41	    pair_row,
42	)
43	from src.polaris_graph.agents.nli_verifier import verify_evidence_nli  # noqa: E402
44	
45	_SEG = pysbd.Segmenter(language="en", clean=False)
46	
47	
48	def _join_knowledge(knowledge) -> str:
49	    return " ".join(knowledge) if isinstance(knowledge, list) else str(knowledge)
50	
51	
52	def _split_claims(answer: str) -> list[str]:
53	    return [s.strip() for s in _SEG.segment(answer or "") if s.strip()]
54	
55	
56	async def _verdict_for_candidate(cand, question: str, knowledge: str) -> str:
57	    """Atomize -> claim-by-claim entailment via verify_evidence_nli -> answer verdict.
58	
59	    Aborts the headline (RunGuards) if the NLI model is unavailable (returns []).
60	    """
61	    claims = _split_claims(cand.answer_text)
62	    if not claims:
63	        return "invalid"
64	    evidence = [build_evidence_object(c, question, knowledge, cand) for c in claims]
65	    url_content_map = {ev["source_url"]: knowledge for ev in evidence}
66	    results = await verify_evidence_nli(evidence, url_content_map, research_query="")
67	    if not results:
68	        RunGuards(nli_model_available=False).assert_headline_valid()  # raises
69	    flags = [bool(r.get("is_faithful")) for r in results]
70	    return aggregate_answer_verdict(flags)
71	
72	
73	async def run(split: str, limit: int, negative_control: bool) -> dict:
74	    from datasets import load_dataset
75	
76	    RunGuards(nli_model_available=True, negative_control=negative_control).assert_headline_valid() if not negative_control else None
77	
78	    ds = load_dataset("UTAustin-AIHealth/MedHallu", split, split="train")
79	    n = min(limit, len(ds)) if limit else len(ds)
80	    rows = [ds[i] for i in range(n)]
81	
82	    # Negative control (Codex): shuffle Knowledge across rows; ground-truth support should
83	    # COLLAPSE. If F1 stays high, the detector is using artifacts/memorized labels, not the packet.
84	    if negative_control:
85	        shuffled = [_join_knowledge(r["Knowledge"]) for r in rows]
86	        random.Random(0xC0DEX).shuffle(shuffled)
87	
88	    conf = Confusion()
89	    per_difficulty: dict[str, Confusion] = {}
90	    for i, r in enumerate(rows):
91	        question = r["Question"]
92	        knowledge = shuffled[i] if negative_control else _join_knowledge(r["Knowledge"])
93	        difficulty = str(r.get("Difficulty Level", "unknown"))
94	        row = {
95	            "row_id": f"{split}:{i}",
96	            "question": question,
97	            "knowledge": _join_knowledge(r["Knowledge"]),  # real knowledge for source-isolation guard
98	            "ground_truth": r["Ground Truth"],
99	            "hallucinated_answer": r["Hallucinated Answer"],
100	        }
101	        for cand in pair_row(row, split):
102	            verdict = await _verdict_for_candidate(cand, question, knowledge)
103	            add_prediction(conf, verdict, cand.gold_hallucinated)
104	            d = per_difficulty.setdefault(difficulty, Confusion())
105	            add_prediction(d, verdict, cand.gold_hallucinated)
106	
107	    return {
108	        "split": split,
109	        "rows_scored": n,
110	        "candidates": expected_candidate_count(n, 0)["pqa_labeled"] if split == "pqa_labeled" else n * 2,
111	        "negative_control": negative_control,
112	        "nli_model": os.environ["PG_NLI_MODEL"],
113	        "threshold": float(os.environ["PG_FAITHFULNESS_NLI_THRESHOLD"]),
114	        "overall": metrics(conf),
115	        "by_difficulty": {k: metrics(v) for k, v in sorted(per_difficulty.items())},
116	        "timestamp_utc": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
117	        "scope_label": (
118	            "POLARIS entailment-verification layer as a MedHallu detector. Positive class = "
119	            "hallucinated. Verifier: MiniCheck flan-t5-large, threshold 0.65, no retrieval, no "
120	            "strict provenance, no LLM fallback. NOT the full POLARIS provenance pipeline."
121	        ),
122	    }
123	
124	
125	def main() -> None:
126	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser()
127	    ap.add_argument("--split", default="pqa_labeled", choices=["pqa_labeled", "pqa_artificial"])
128	    ap.add_argument("--limit", type=int, default=10)
129	    ap.add_argument("--negative-control", action="store_true")
130	    ap.add_argument("--out", default="")
131	    args = ap.parse_args()
132	
133	    result = asyncio.run(run(args.split, args.limit, args.negative_control))
134	    out = Path(args.out) if args.out else Path(
135	        f"outputs/dr_benchmark/medhallu_{args.split}_n{args.limit}"
136	        f"{'_negctrl' if args.negative_control else ''}.json"
137	    )
138	    out.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
139	    out.write_text(json.dumps(result, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")
140	    print(json.dumps(result, indent=2))
141	    print(f"\nwrote {out}")
142	
143	
144	if __name__ == "__main__":
145	    main()
146

1	"""Targeted single-source MAP probe (#1217 Bug B diagnostic).
2	
3	Runs the production ``distill_section_evidence`` on ONE evidence source for ONE
4	section, with ``PG_DISTILL_DEBUG=1`` reject tracing on, and dumps the per-source
5	``CoverageRow`` (status + n_findings + "N proposed, M validated" reason) plus every
6	validated finding's claim/quote. This answers, for the one source that matters
7	(the CDC safety source [4] whose findings legacy mined but the distill ledger
8	dropped): did the MAP propose findings, and if so which validation STEP killed
9	them — without running a full 8-source A/B.
10	
11	Faithfulness: this ORCHESTRATES the real production distiller (zero gate drift).
12	The live MAP call is gated behind ``--live`` (default OFF) so the module imports
13	+ unit-tests offline. One source = ~one LLM call (~1 cent).
14	
15	Usage (live, authorized spend, on the OVH VM):
16	    PG_DISTILL_DEBUG=1 python scripts/dr_benchmark/probe_source_map.py --live \
17	        --pool <evidence_pool.json> \
18	        --evidence-id probiotic_immunocompromised_contraindication \
19	        --section-title "Safety and contraindications" \
20	        --section-focus "Safety, adverse events, and contraindications of the intervention"
21	"""
22	
23	from __future__ import annotations
24	
25	import argparse
26	import asyncio
27	import json
28	import os
29	import sys
30	from pathlib import Path
31	from typing import Any, Optional
32	
33	_REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2]
34	if str(_REPO_ROOT) not in sys.path:
35	    sys.path.insert(0, str(_REPO_ROOT))
36	
37	
38	def load_pool(path: str | Path) -> dict[str, dict[str, Any]]:
39	    raw = json.loads(Path(path).read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
40	    rows = raw if isinstance(raw, list) else list(raw.values())
41	    pool: dict[str, dict[str, Any]] = {}
42	    for i, row in enumerate(rows):
43	        eid = row.get("evidence_id")
44	        if not eid:
45	            raise ValueError(f"evidence row {i} has no evidence_id")
46	        pool[eid] = row
47	    return pool
48	
49	
50	async def run_probe(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
51	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.evidence_distiller import distill_section_evidence
52	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.multi_section_generator import SectionPlan
53	
54	    pool = load_pool(args.pool)
55	    if args.evidence_id not in pool:
56	        raise SystemExit(f"evidence_id {args.evidence_id!r} not in pool ({len(pool)} rows)")
57	    row = pool[args.evidence_id]
58	    dq = row.get("direct_quote") or row.get("text") or ""
59	    print(f"[probe] source={args.evidence_id} dq_len={len(dq)} model={args.model}")
60	
61	    section = SectionPlan(title=args.section_title, focus=args.section_focus,
62	                          ev_ids=[args.evidence_id])
63	    dist = await distill_section_evidence(
64	        section, [row], pool, model=args.model, max_parallel=1,
65	    )
66	
67	    print(f"\n[probe] === COVERAGE ({len(dist.coverage)} row(s)) ===")
68	    for c in dist.coverage:
69	        print(f"  status={getattr(c,'status','?')} n_findings={getattr(c,'n_findings','?')} "
70	              f"reason={getattr(c,'reason','')!r}")
71	    print(f"\n[probe] === VALIDATED FINDINGS ({len(dist.findings)}) ===")
72	    for f in dist.findings:
73	        print(f"  - claim={f.claim!r}")
74	        print(f"    numbers={f.numbers} atom_ids={f.atom_ids} quote={f.support_quote[:120]!r}")
75	    if not dist.findings:
76	        print("  (none — see the [DISTILL_DEBUG] REJECT lines above for the killing step)")
77	    return 0
78	
79	
80	def build_arg_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
81	    p = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Single-source MAP probe (#1217 Bug B).")
82	    p.add_argument("--live", action="store_true",
83	                   help="Make a REAL MAP LLM call (authorized spend). Default OFF.")
84	    p.add_argument("--pool", type=str, required=False,
85	                   default="outputs/audits/I-perm-010/run_drb76_iter2/drb_76_gut_microbiota_crc/evidence_pool.json")
86	    p.add_argument("--evidence-id", type=str, default="probiotic_immunocompromised_contraindication")
87	    p.add_argument("--section-title", type=str, default="Safety and contraindications")
88	    p.add_argument("--section-focus", type=str,
89	                   default="Safety, adverse events, and contraindications of the intervention")
90	    p.add_argument("--model", type=str, default=os.getenv("PG_GENERATOR_MODEL", "deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro"))
91	    return p
92	
93	
94	def main(argv: Optional[list[str]] = None) -> int:
95	    args = build_arg_parser().parse_args(argv)
96	    if not args.live:
97	        print("probe_source_map: refusing to run the LIVE MAP call without --live "
98	              "(it makes a real billable LLM call). Re-run with --live on the OVH VM.")
99	        return 0
100	    return asyncio.run(run_probe(args))
101	
102	
103	if __name__ == "__main__":
104	    raise SystemExit(main())
